provenance.source_corpus: Climatic variability alters the outcome of long‐term community assembly

source_spans.text: Conversion of degraded grassland back to native-dominated grassland was possible, but only with the removal of the exotic dominant and seed additions during years that were suitably wet for establishment.
Herbicide (glyphosate) was applied using a combination of methods that increased in intensity over time in an attempt to eliminate A. cristatum.
plots seeded in 1995 and herbicide-treated... had significantly lower A. cristatum cover compared to herbicide-treated but unseeded plots
plots seeded in 1995 and herbicide-treated... had significantly higher B. gracilis cover
Total species richness, however, was significantly higher in plots treated with herbicide until 2003
The establishment success of B. gracilis was associated with variations in climate conditions in the spring and summer of the establishment years
In the year of highest establishment (1995), monthly rainfall averages exceeded the 95% CIs in every month from April until October except May
All seeded grasses, other than B. gracilis, were mostly absent by 2004
